sections.paragraph:
- Original: Ῥόδος

- Transliteration: Rhodos

- Phonetic: hrod'-os

- Definition: Rhodes = "rosy"

1. a well known island of the Cyclades opposite Caria and Lycia, with a Rhodes as the capital city

- Origin: probably from rhodon (a rose)

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Location

- Strong's: Probably from ῥόδον rhodon (a rose); Rhodus an island of the mediterranean: - Rhodes.
